name: The Leverage Loop
niche: AI tools & productivity for professionals and business
target_audience: Knowledge workers, founders, and operators deciding which AI tools are worth paying for — the buyer with a budget and a stack to defend, choosing where to spend. Not beginners looking for 'what is ChatGPT', and not engineers looking for model internals — practitioners weighing real cost against real payoff.
channel_pov: Honest, data-backed verdicts on AI tools: which are actually worth the money, which are overhyped, and which to skip - judged on real pricing, real-user reviews, and hands-on feature comparison, with no affiliate bias.
voice: calm, specific, slightly skeptical; concrete numbers and tradeoffs over adjectives; talks to a professional deciding where to spend, not a hype audience.
authenticity_requirements: Original synthesis across multiple sources with a clear point of view — never a single-source rewrite or a feature-list read-out.
Every factual claim, statistic, quote, and citation must be real and checkable; never fabricate. Sources carry verified:false until a human confirms them.
Flag fast-changing facts (tool pricing, model versions, feature lists, free-tier limits) for human verification before publish — this niche goes stale fast.
Materially specific: real examples, real tool behavior, real tradeoffs — not generic filler.
Stay clear of the July-2025 'inauthentic / mass-produced content' demonetization rules: human review gate before every publish, transformative analysis, consistent on-channel voice.
